About Ember & Oak · Neighborhood Coffee Since 2018

Made slowly.
Served warmly.

Ember & Oak is a neighborhood coffee shop built for unhurried mornings, careful craft, and the familiar feeling of being welcomed by name.

Read Our Story → Hours & Location
6

years of daily service

14

rotating single origins

1

very beloved corner shop

House Favorite

Brown sugar oat latte

Roast Style

Balanced, sweet, never rushed

Our Story

A small shop with a long table.

We opened Ember & Oak with twelve seats, one espresso machine, and a simple belief: coffee tastes better when people feel cared for.

Our promise: every drink is prepared with attention, every pastry is chosen with intention, and every guest gets the kind of welcome that makes the day feel lighter.

The name Ember & Oak comes from the way we think about hospitality. Ember is warmth that lasts. Oak is something steady, familiar, and rooted. Together, they describe the space we try to create every day: a bright room where regulars gather, first-time visitors settle in quickly, and the pace of the neighborhood slows down for a few good minutes.

Our founders started behind the bar, learning how small details change everything: water temperature, roast age, milk texture, the way a cup is handed across the counter. Today, that care still shapes our menu, our sourcing, our training, and the playlists humming in the background.

2018

Opened with a tiny bar and a big neighborhood welcome.

2021

Expanded our bakery program with local makers.

2026

Still serving every cup like it matters, because it does.

What We Believe

Good coffee is only half the work.

Our bar is guided by a few everyday practices that keep the experience consistent, kind, and genuinely local.

1
Hospitality first

We train our team to notice people before orders, because the best coffee shop moments start with a genuine welcome.

2
Craft without ego

We care deeply about extraction, roast profiles, and technique, but we explain everything in plain language.

3
Local partnerships

Our pastry case, flowers, ceramics, and community boards are shaped by makers and neighbors close to home.

4
Seasonal curiosity

We rotate coffees, syrups, and small menu specials so regulars always have something new to discover.

5
Responsible sourcing

We prioritize roasters and importers who share transparent farm relationships and pay attention to long-term sustainability.

6
Room for everyone

Solo readers, laptop regulars, stroller crews, first dates, and post-shift friends all belong at our tables.

Little detail, big difference: We taste espresso throughout the day and adjust the grind as temperature, humidity, and roast age change.

Coffee, Carefully Chosen

We chase sweetness, balance, and clarity.

Our menu is built around coffees that are expressive but approachable: bright enough to be memorable, balanced enough to drink every day.

☕

Espresso with structure

Our house espresso is chosen for chocolate, caramel, and toasted nut notes that hold up beautifully with milk and shine on their own.

🌿

Single origins in rotation

We feature rotating lots from small producers and cooperatives, highlighting fruit, florals, spice, and origin character.

🥐

Food that fits the cup

Our pastry partners bake in small batches, with buttery, savory, gluten-conscious, and plant-forward options on the board.

Current Notes

Candied orange, cocoa nib, brown sugar, toasted almond.

Ask Us About

Dialing in at home, choosing beans, and finding your favorite brew method.

Behind the Bar

The people who know your order.

Our team is made of baristas, bakers, artists, students, parents, musicians, and morning people by necessity.

Founder · Head Barista

Maya Rivera

Maya opened Ember & Oak after years of coffee training and a lifelong habit of turning strangers into regulars.

Roasting Lead

Theo Bennett

Theo keeps our coffee program balanced, curious, and consistent from first dial-in to the last afternoon pour-over.

Community Host

Lena Cho

Lena curates our local art wall, open-mic nights, donation drives, and the community board by the front door.

In the Neighborhood

More than a morning stop.

We use our little corner to make space for local creativity, mutual aid, and the small rituals that help a neighborhood feel like home.

Local art wall

A rotating monthly wall featuring artists within a few miles of the shop.

Neighborhood table

A large shared table reserved for conversation, reading, and low-pressure connection.

Pay-it-forward board

Guests can leave a coffee for someone who needs one, no questions asked.

Common Questions

Yes. We offer free Wi-Fi, several outlets, and a mix of small tables and bar seats. We keep the largest table laptop-free during weekend brunch hours so groups can gather.

Absolutely. Oat milk is our most popular pairing, and we also keep almond milk available. Our team can recommend drinks that work especially well without dairy.

Yes. We stock our house espresso, rotating filter coffees, and seasonal limited releases. Ask us to grind a bag for your home setup.

We host small gatherings, cuppings, and community events after hours. Stop by the counter and tell us what you have in mind.

What Regulars Say

The kind words that keep us going.

We are grateful for every person who makes Ember & Oak part of their commute, walk, study session, catch-up, or quiet ritual.

Come By Soon

Your table is waiting.

Stop in for espresso, pour-over, seasonal drinks, fresh pastries, and a little room to breathe.

Hours

Mon–Fri · 7am–5pm

Sat–Sun · 8am–4pm

124 Maple Street
Old Town District

Stay in the Loop

Seasonal drinks, events, and fresh bean drops.

Join our small newsletter for monthly notes from the shop. No spam, just good coffee news.
